question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cachedResponse.getBody()` call where getBody() returns `Buffer.from(this.body, 'base64url')` to cause a buffer over-read or memory disclosure if this.body contains invalid base64url characters (injected via Redis poisoning), potentially leaking memory contents that include other users' financial data?
